summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a classic Fall ’79 show with standout performances like Candyman, Terrapin and the Jack‑Straw / Deal combo.
review: The recording is a pristine soundboard mix that many reviewers call “extremely good” and “as good as it gets,” making every nuance of the band audible. Musically the concert shines – Jerry’s near‑perfect guitar work, Bob Weir’s strong vocals on a range of numbers, Brent’s early‑era keyboard contributions, and a memorable Terrapin followed by a 22‑minute jam make this one of the finer shows of the late‑1979 period.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are 5‑star and overwhelmingly praise both performance and sound quality; the show is described as flawless with only minor subjective notes, while the soundboard source is called excellent.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note crystal‑clear mix, superior to other sources, with no audible flaws or dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical Fall ’79 set but with refreshing changes (e.g., less China>Rider), a strong first set and a solid second set highlighted by an extended Terrapin jam.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Near‑perfect guitar tone; doesn’t stretch out as much as some shows but is very close to flawless.
band_member_comments.Bob: Very strong throughout the night, delivering exceptional vocals on multiple numbers.
band_member_comments.Keys: Early Brent era – still learning the vast songbook but contributes solidly to the performance.
